title: The Magicians of Caprona - Chapter 5 (Fragment 5)
text:
Old Niccolo had his look of a baby about to cry. Aunt Francesca said, βÄ€WeβÄôre not asking this personallyβÄîβÄù

βÄ€But, my dear,βÄù said Chrestomanci, βÄ€donβÄôt you see that I can only do something like this as a personal matter? Please ask me personally. I shanβÄôt let the strict terms of my mission interfere with what I owe my friends.βÄù He smiled then, and his eyes swept around everyone gathered at the great table, very affectionately. He did not seem to exclude Marco. βÄ€So,βÄù he said, βÄ€I think my best plan for the moment is to go on to Rome. I know certain quarters there, where I can get impartial information, which should enable me to pin down this enchanter. At the moment, all we know is that he exists. If IβÄôm lucky, I can prove whether Florence, or Siena, or Pisa is paying himβÄîin which case, they and he can be indicted at the Court of Europe. And if, while IβÄôm at it, I can get Rome, or Naples, to move on CapronaβÄôs behalf, be very sure I shall do it.βÄù

βÄ€Thank you,βÄù said Old Niccolo.

For the rest of supper, they discussed how Chrestomanci could best get to Rome. He would have to go by sea. It seemed that the last stretch of border, between Caprona and Siena, was now closed.

Much later that night, when Paolo and Tonino were on their way to bed, they saw lights in the Scriptorium. They tiptoed along to investigate. Chrestomanci was there with Antonio, Rinaldo and Aunt Francesca, going through spells in the big red books. Everyone was speaking in mutters, but they heard Chrestomanci say, βÄ€This is a sound combination, but itβÄôll need new words.βÄù And on another page, βÄ€Get Elizabeth to put this in English, as a surprise factor.βÄù And again, βÄ€Ignore the tune. The only tune which is going to be any use to you at the moment is the Angel . He canβÄôt block that.βÄù

βÄ€Why just those three?βÄù Tonino whispered.

βÄ€TheyβÄôre best at making new spells,βÄù Paolo whispered back. βÄ€We need new war-spells. It sounds as if the other enchanter knows the old ones.βÄù

They crept to bed with an excited, urgent feeling, and neither of them found it easy to sleep.

Chrestomanci left the next morning before the children went to school. Benvenuto and Old Niccolo escorted him to the gate, one on either side, and the entire Casa gathered to wave him off. Things felt both flat and worrying once he was gone. That day, there was a great deal of talk of war at school. The teachers whispered together. Two had left, to join the Reserves. Rumors went around the classes. Someone told Tonino that war would be declared next Sunday, so that it would be a Holy War. Someone else told Paolo that all the Reserves had been issued with two left boots, so that they would not be able to fight. There was no truth in these things. It was just that everyone now knew that war was coming.

The boys hurried home, anxious for some real news. As usual, Benvenuto leaped off his waterbutt. While Tonino was enjoying BenvenutoβÄôs undivided attention again, Elizabeth called from the gallery, βÄ€Tonino! SomeoneβÄôs sent you a parcel.βÄù

Tonino and Benvenuto sprang for the gallery stairs, highly excited. Tonino had never had a parcel before. But before he got anywhere near it, he was seized on by Aunt Maria, Rosa and Uncle Lorenzo. They seized on all the children who could write and hurried them to the dining room. This had been set up as another Scriptorium. By each chair was a special pen, a bottle of red war-ink and a pile of strips of paper. There the children were kept busy fully two hours, copying the same war-charm, again and again. Tonino had never been so frustrated in his life. He did not even know what shape his parcel was. He was not the only one to feel frustrated.

vocabulary.definition: In a way that shows fondness or tenderness.
vocabulary.example: βÄù He smiled then, and his eyes swept around everyone gathered at the great table, very affectionately.

vocabulary.definition: A grouping or joining of two or more things.
vocabulary.example: Everyone was speaking in mutters, but they heard Chrestomanci say, βÄ€This is a sound combination, but itβÄôll need new words.

vocabulary.definition: An official or formal announcement.
vocabulary.example: Someone told Tonino that war would be declared next Sunday, so that it would be a Holy War.

vocabulary.definition: A person who practices magic or sorcery.
vocabulary.example: I know certain quarters there, where I can get impartial information, which should enable me to pin down this enchanter.

vocabulary.definition: To accompany someone for protection or courtesy.
vocabulary.example: Benvenuto and Old Niccolo escorted him to the gate, one on either side, and the entire Casa gathered to wave him off.

vocabulary.definition: To deny someone access or prevent inclusion.
vocabulary.example: He did not seem to exclude Marco.

vocabulary.definition: Feeling annoyed or upset due to inability to achieve something.
vocabulary.example: Tonino had never been so frustrated in his life.

vocabulary.definition: To come together in a group.
vocabulary.example: βÄù He smiled then, and his eyes swept around everyone gathered at the great table, very affectionately.

vocabulary.definition: To move or act quickly.
vocabulary.example: The boys hurried home, anxious for some real news.

vocabulary.definition: Treating all rivals or disputants equally; fair and just.
vocabulary.example: I know certain quarters there, where I can get impartial information, which should enable me to pin down this enchanter.

vocabulary.definition: Information that is unbiased and objective.
vocabulary.example: I know certain quarters there, where I can get impartial information, which should enable me to pin down this enchanter.

vocabulary.definition: Formally accuse or charge with a serious crime.
vocabulary.example: If IβÄôm lucky, I can prove whether Florence, or Siena, or Pisa is paying himβÄîin which case, they and he can be indicted at the Court of Europe.

vocabulary.definition: To get involved in and possibly cause disruption.
vocabulary.example: I shanβÄôt let the strict terms of my mission interfere with what I owe my friends.

vocabulary.definition: To carry out a systematic or formal inquiry.
vocabulary.example: They tiptoed along to investigate.

vocabulary.definition: To supply or distribute something officially.
vocabulary.example: Someone else told Paolo that all the Reserves had been issued with two left boots, so that they would not be able to fight.

vocabulary.definition: An important task or duty assigned.
vocabulary.example: I shanβÄôt let the strict terms of my mission interfere with what I owe my friends.

vocabulary.definition: To speak quietly and indistinctly.
vocabulary.example: Everyone was speaking in mutters, but they heard Chrestomanci say, βÄ€This is a sound combination, but itβÄôll need new words.

vocabulary.definition: To identify or locate something precisely.
vocabulary.example: I know certain quarters there, where I can get impartial information, which should enable me to pin down this enchanter.

vocabulary.definition: Districts or areas in a town or city.
vocabulary.example: I know certain quarters there, where I can get impartial information, which should enable me to pin down this enchanter.

vocabulary.definition: Military personnel not in active service but available if needed.
vocabulary.example: Two had left, to join the Reserves.

vocabulary.definition: An element that causes unexpectedness or astonishment.
vocabulary.example: βÄù And on another page, βÄ€Get Elizabeth to put this in English, as a surprise factor.

vocabulary.definition: To walk quietly and carefully on the toes.
vocabulary.example: They tiptoed along to investigate.

vocabulary.definition: Requiring immediate action or attention.
vocabulary.example:
βÄù

They crept to bed with an excited, urgent feeling, and neither of them found it easy to sleep.

vocabulary.definition: Magical spells intended for use in war or combat.
vocabulary.example: βÄ€We need new war-spells.

vocabulary.definition: To speak very softly using one's breath.
vocabulary.example: βÄù Tonino whispered.
